answers: A pricing strategy is the method a business uses to set prices for its products or services, considering costs, competition, market demand, and perceived value.
Pricing strategy holo tomar product ba service er dam kivabe set korba tar plan. Cost, competition, ar customer willingness to pay - shob consider korte hoy.
Common pricing strategies include cost-plus pricing, competitive pricing, value-based pricing, penetration pricing (low to enter market), and premium pricing (high for luxury).
The best pricing strategy depends on your market. Test different prices, understand your costs, research competitors, know your customer's willingness to pay, and don't undervalue your work.
